title: LightCycler 480 Probes qPCR
description: Roche probe-based 20 uL qPCR: 95 C pre-incubation, 45 cycles, short 72 C extension, no melt curve.

steps.name: Annealing (set ~5 C below primer Tm; 60 C typical)

payload.notes: Probe-based (hydrolysis probe) chemistry, so there is no melt curve. Roche pools the forward and reverse primers plus probe into a single 10X mix; primers 0.3-1 uM final each, hydrolysis probe 0.05-0.2 uM final. Annealing temperature is primer-dependent (set ~5 C below the calculated Tm; 60 C is a common starting point). Fluorescence is read during the annealing step, so the 72 C extension is only ~1 sec. 45 cycles suit most assays; 40 suffice for optimized assays. Cool to 40 C at the end. Source: Roche LightCycler 480 Probes Master pack insert, https://lifescience.roche.com/global/en/products/others/lightcycler-480-probes-master-358111.html
